segments.text: Measles is one of the most contagious infectious diseases and remains a leading cause of death, particularly among young children, especially in areas with low rates of vaccination. Measles is also called rubeola, which can easily get confused with German measles, which is also called rubella. Similar sounding names but very different viruses. Regular measles is caused by the measles virus. Seriously. The species is the measles virus of the genus Morbilla virus and family Paramyxoveridae.

The species is the measles virus of the genus Morbilla virus and family Paramyxoveridae. The reason why this guy's so contagious is that it's airborne and spreads via tiny liquid particles that get flung into the air when someone sneezes or coughs, and can live for up to two hours in that airspace or nearby surfaces if someone breathes in that air or touches a surface and then touches their eyes, their nose, or their mouth.
